methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in AI & Software (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 38 same-sector peers exist in AI & Software, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
HUMAIN is a sovereign-stage AI & Software company backed by PIF, having raised $40B+ in capitalized commitments. The company operates within a fragmented AI & Software sector comprising 38 peer companies, though the comparable universe at HUMAIN's scale and stage is severely constrained: only 1 public comparable (Palantir; PLTR) and 8 identified private peers including OpenAI, xAI, Anthropic, and Databricks.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to this analysis. The target lacks sufficient narrow-stage comparables—zero same-stage, zero adjacent-stage, and zero similar-valuation-tier peers meet standard filtering thresholds. A fallback to all-sector median multiples would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity in business model, profitability, and capital deployment within the broader AI & Software universe.

The analysis cannot produce a triangulated valuation range without methodologically sound comparable datasets or disclosed cash-flow, revenue, or margin metrics for HUMAIN itself. Key uncertainties include: the lack of standardized financial disclosure for sovereign-stage allocations; the absence of precedent transactions at this scale in frontier AI; and the structural differences between Palantir's defense-intelligence positioning and HUMAIN's undisclosed operational focus. Further valuation work would require either expansion of the private peer set to ≥3 validated comparable transactions, or access to HUMAIN's financial or revenue guidance.
